questions.stem_en: Read the sentence and identify the word class of the underlined word: She read the book *quickly*.
questions.stem_fa: جمله را بخوانید و طبقهٔ واژگانی واژهٔ مشخص‌شده را تعیین کنید: She read the book *quickly*.
questions.explanation_en: 'Quickly' describes HOW she read — it modifies the verb 'read', so it is an adverb (many, not all, end in -ly). A is wrong: an adjective describes a noun (e.g. a *quick* reader), but here there is no noun being described. B is wrong: the verb is 'read', not 'quickly'.
questions.explanation_fa: «Quickly» می‌گوید او چگونه خواند — فعل «read» را توصیف می‌کند، پس قید (adverb) است (بسیاری، نه همه، به -ly ختم می‌شوند). گزینهٔ A غلط است: صفت اسم را توصیف می‌کند (مثل a *quick* reader)، اما اینجا اسمی توصیف نمی‌شود. گزینهٔ B غلط است: فعلِ جمله «read» است، نه «quickly».

questions.stem_en: Which tense is used here? 'I have lived in Tehran for ten years.'
questions.stem_fa: اینجا کدام زمان فعل به‌کار رفته است؟ 'I have lived in Tehran for ten years.'
questions.explanation_en: 'have + past participle (lived)' is the present perfect, used for an action that started in the past and continues now ('for ten years'). A (past simple) would be 'I lived' and suggests it is finished. C (present continuous) is 'am living' and describes a temporary action in progress, not a ten-year span with 'for'.
questions.explanation_fa: ساختار «have + اسم مفعول (lived)» حال کامل (present perfect) است که برای کنشی به‌کار می‌رود که در گذشته شروع شده و تا حالا ادامه دارد («for ten years»). گزینهٔ A (گذشتهٔ ساده) می‌شد «I lived» و یعنی تمام شده. گزینهٔ C (حال استمراری) «am living» است و کنشی موقت و در حال انجام را نشان می‌دهد، نه بازهٔ ده‌ساله با «for».

questions.stem_en: Identify the word class of 'between' in: 'The ball is *between* the chairs.'
questions.stem_fa: طبقهٔ واژگانی «between» را تعیین کنید: 'The ball is *between* the chairs.'
questions.explanation_en: 'Between' shows the position/relationship of the ball to the chairs, so it is a preposition. A is wrong: a conjunction joins clauses or words (and, but, because). C is wrong: a pronoun replaces a noun (it, they) — 'between' does not replace anything.
questions.explanation_fa: «Between» جایگاه/رابطهٔ توپ نسبت به صندلی‌ها را نشان می‌دهد، پس حرف اضافه (preposition) است. گزینهٔ A غلط است: حرف ربط واژه‌ها یا بندها را به هم می‌پیوندد (and, but, because). گزینهٔ C غلط است: ضمیر جای اسم می‌نشیند (it, they) — «between» جای چیزی نمی‌نشیند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام واژه یک تعیین‌کننده (determiner) است؟
questions.explanation_en: 'The' is a determiner — it comes before a noun to identify it (the book). A 'happy' is an adjective (describes a noun). C 'run' is a verb (an action). Determiners include articles (a/an/the), possessives (my, your) and demonstratives (this, that).
questions.explanation_fa: «The» تعیین‌کننده است — پیش از اسم می‌آید تا آن را معرفی کند (the book). گزینهٔ A یعنی «happy» صفت است (اسم را توصیف می‌کند). گزینهٔ C یعنی «run» فعل است (کنش). تعیین‌کننده‌ها شامل حروف تعریف (a/an/the)، ملکی‌ها (my, your) و اشاره‌ای‌ها (this, that) هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام واژه به‌طور طبیعی با «heavy» هم‌آیی (collocation) دارد؟
questions.explanation_en: 'Heavy rain' is a fixed, natural word partnership (collocation). We do not say 'heavy sun' (we say 'strong sun') or 'heavy wind' (we say 'strong wind'). Collocations must be learned as chunks, not worked out by rules.
questions.explanation_fa: «Heavy rain» یک هم‌نشینی طبیعی و تثبیت‌شده (collocation) است. نمی‌گوییم «heavy sun» (می‌گوییم «strong sun») یا «heavy wind» (می‌گوییم «strong wind»). هم‌آیی‌ها را باید به‌صورت بستهٔ آماده (chunk) یاد گرفت، نه با قاعده ساخت.

questions.stem_en: In the word 'unhappy', what does the prefix 'un-' do?
questions.stem_fa: در واژهٔ «unhappy»، پیشوند «un-» چه نقشی دارد؟
questions.explanation_en: 'un-' is a prefix that gives a negative/opposite meaning: happy -> unhappy (= not happy). B is wrong: tense is shown by verb endings/forms, not 'un-'. C is wrong: plurals are usually shown by '-s'. A prefix is an affix added at the START of a word.
questions.explanation_fa: «un-» پیشوندی است که معنای منفی/متضاد می‌دهد: happy ← unhappy (یعنی «نه شاد»). گزینهٔ B غلط است: زمان با پایانه/شکل فعل نشان داده می‌شود، نه «un-». گزینهٔ C غلط است: جمع معمولاً با «-s» نشان داده می‌شود. پیشوند، وندی است که به ابتدای واژه افزوده می‌شود.

questions.stem_en: Which item is a phrasal verb?
questions.stem_fa: کدام مورد یک فعل عبارتی (phrasal verb) است؟
questions.explanation_en: 'Look after' = verb + particle, with a meaning (take care of) that is not the sum of its parts — a phrasal verb. B 'beautiful' is an adjective; C 'quickly' is an adverb. Phrasal verbs are multi-word lexical items and must be learned as units.
questions.explanation_fa: «Look after» = فعل + جزء (particle) با معنایی (مراقبت کردن) که از جمع اجزایش به دست نمی‌آید — یک فعل عبارتی است. گزینهٔ B «beautiful» صفت و گزینهٔ C «quickly» قید است. افعال عبارتی واحدهای واژگانی چندکلمه‌ای‌اند و باید به‌صورت واحد یاد گرفته شوند.

questions.stem_en: 'Blackboard' (black + board) is an example of a:
questions.stem_fa: «Blackboard» (black + board) نمونه‌ای است از:
questions.explanation_en: Joining two words to make a new one (black + board) creates a compound — here a compound noun. B is wrong: an idiom is a fixed expression whose meaning is not literal (e.g. 'kick the bucket'). C is wrong: synonyms are words with similar meaning (big/large).
questions.explanation_fa: پیوند دو واژه برای ساختن واژه‌ای جدید (black + board) یک واژهٔ مرکب (compound) می‌سازد — اینجا اسم مرکب. گزینهٔ B غلط است: اصطلاح عبارتی ثابت است که معنایش تحت‌اللفظی نیست (مثل «kick the bucket»). گزینهٔ C غلط است: مترادف‌ها واژه‌هایی با معنای مشابه‌اند (big/large).

questions.stem_en: A learner says 'I goed home'. This is best described as:
questions.stem_fa: یک زبان‌آموز می‌گوید «I goed home». این مورد را بهتر است چگونه توصیف کرد:

options.text_en: a slip (knows the rule, careless error)

questions.explanation_en: The learner has applied the regular '-ed' past rule to an irregular verb (go -> went), so it is an overgeneralisation — a developmental error showing learning is happening. A is wrong: a slip is when a learner who KNOWS the form makes a careless one-off mistake and can self-correct. C is wrong: interference would be an error caused by the first language, not by over-applying an English rule.
questions.explanation_fa: زبان‌آموز قاعدهٔ منظمِ گذشته با «-ed» را روی یک فعل بی‌قاعده (go ← went) به‌کار برده، پس این تعمیم افراطی (overgeneralisation) است — خطایی تحولی که نشان می‌دهد یادگیری در حال رخ دادن است. گزینهٔ A غلط است: لغزش وقتی است که زبان‌آموزی که شکل را می‌داند یک اشتباه بی‌دقتی موردی می‌کند و می‌تواند خودش اصلاح کند. گزینهٔ C غلط است: تداخل، خطایی است که از زبان اول ناشی می‌شود، نه از به‌کار بستن افراطیِ یک قاعدهٔ انگلیسی.

questions.stem_en: Learners do a group task while the teacher stands back and only helps when asked. Which role is the teacher mainly taking?
questions.stem_fa: زبان‌آموزان تکلیف گروهی انجام می‌دهند و معلم کنار می‌ایستد و فقط وقتی از او خواسته شود کمک می‌کند. معلم بیشتر کدام نقش را دارد؟
questions.explanation_en: Standing back and supporting only when needed is the facilitator role — it maximises learner autonomy and talking time. A (controller) leads from the front and directs everything. C (assessor) focuses on judging/marking performance, which is not what is happening here.
questions.explanation_fa: کنار ایستادن و کمک فقط هنگام نیاز، نقش تسهیل‌گر (facilitator) است — استقلال زبان‌آموز و زمان صحبت او را بیشینه می‌کند. گزینهٔ A (کنترل‌کننده) از جلو هدایت می‌کند و همه‌چیز را خودش می‌گرداند. گزینهٔ C (ارزیاب) بر قضاوت/نمره‌دادن تمرکز دارد که اینجا رخ نمی‌دهد.

questions.stem_en: During a freer fluency speaking activity, the most appropriate approach to correction is usually to:
questions.stem_fa: در یک فعالیت گفتاریِ آزادِ مبتنی بر روانی، مناسب‌ترین رویکرد به تصحیح معمولاً این است که:

options.text_fa: هر خطا را فوراً تصحیح کنیم

options.text_en: note errors and give delayed feedback afterwards
options.text_fa: خطاها را یادداشت و بازخورد را با تأخیر بعد بدهیم
کل زبان را کاملاً نادیده بگیریم

questions.explanation_en: In fluency activities the aim is communication, so immediate correction (A) interrupts the flow and discourages learners; the usual choice is to monitor, note errors and give delayed feedback afterwards (B). C is wrong: ignoring everything wastes useful feedback — the teacher still records errors to deal with later.
questions.explanation_fa: در فعالیت‌های روانی هدف، ارتباط است؛ پس تصحیح فوری (گزینهٔ A) جریان را قطع می‌کند و زبان‌آموز را دلسرد می‌کند؛ انتخاب رایج این است که معلم نظارت کند، خطاها را یادداشت و بازخورد را با تأخیر بدهد (گزینهٔ B). گزینهٔ C غلط است: نادیده‌گرفتن کامل، بازخورد مفید را هدر می‌دهد — معلم همچنان خطاها را برای رسیدگی بعدی ثبت می‌کند.

questions.stem_en: An approach in which learners complete a meaningful task and language is dealt with afterwards is:
questions.stem_fa: رویکردی که در آن زبان‌آموزان یک تکلیف معنادار را کامل می‌کنند و زبان بعد از آن بررسی می‌شود، چه نام دارد؟
questions.explanation_en: Task-Based Learning puts a communicative task first and focuses on language that arises from it afterwards. A (Grammar-Translation) centres on translating sentences and explicit grammar rules. C (Audiolingualism) is based on drills and habit formation, not on meaningful tasks.
questions.explanation_fa: یادگیری مبتنی بر تکلیف ابتدا یک تکلیف ارتباطی را قرار می‌دهد و سپس روی زبانی که از آن برمی‌خیزد تمرکز می‌کند. گزینهٔ A (دستور-ترجمه) بر ترجمهٔ جمله‌ها و قواعد صریح دستوری متمرکز است. گزینهٔ C (شنیداری-گفتاری) بر تمرین‌های تکراری و عادت‌سازی استوار است، نه تکالیف معنادار.

questions.stem_en: Which of these is a minimal pair?
questions.stem_fa: کدام‌یک یک جفت کمینه (minimal pair) است؟
questions.explanation_en: A minimal pair is two words that differ in only ONE phoneme: ship and sheep differ only in the vowel sound. B differ in meaning, not a single sound. C differ by a whole grammatical ending (-ing), not one phoneme.
questions.explanation_fa: جفت کمینه دو واژه است که فقط در یک واج فرق دارند: ship و sheep تنها در صدای مصوت تفاوت دارند. گزینهٔ B در معنا فرق دارند نه در یک صدا. گزینهٔ C با یک پایانهٔ دستوری کامل (-ing) فرق دارند، نه یک واج.

questions.stem_en: The strong (most prominent) syllable in a word is its ___.
questions.stem_fa: هجای قوی (برجسته‌ترین) در یک واژه، ___ آن است.
questions.explanation_en: Word stress is the syllable said with most prominence (e.g. PHOtograph). A phoneme is a single sound, not a syllable's prominence. Intonation is the rise and fall of the voice across an utterance, not the stressed syllable.
questions.explanation_fa: تکیهٔ واژه همان هجایی است که با بیشترین برجستگی گفته می‌شود (مثل PHOtograph). واج یک صدای منفرد است، نه برجستگیِ هجا. آهنگ کلام بالا و پایین‌رفتن صدا در طول گفته است، نه هجای تکیه‌دار.

questions.stem_en: 'Could you open the window, please?' — which function does this perform?
questions.stem_fa: «Could you open the window, please?» — این جمله کدام کارکرد را انجام می‌دهد؟
questions.explanation_en: The speaker is asking someone to do something — this is requesting. Apologising would be 'I'm sorry...'; inviting would be 'Would you like to come...?'.
questions.explanation_fa: گوینده از کسی می‌خواهد کاری انجام دهد — این «درخواست» است. عذرخواهی می‌شد «I'm sorry...» و دعوت می‌شد «Would you like to come...?».

questions.stem_en: Two different phrases that perform the same function are called different ___.
questions.stem_fa: دو عبارت متفاوت که یک کارکرد واحد را انجام می‌دهند، ___ مختلف نامیده می‌شوند.
questions.explanation_en: The actual phrases used to express a function are its exponents. Phonemes are sounds. Register is the level of formality, not the phrase itself.
questions.explanation_fa: عبارت‌هایی که برای بیان یک کارکرد به‌کار می‌روند «نمود/exponent» آن‌اند. واج‌ها صدا هستند. register سطح رسمی‌بودن است، نه خودِ عبارت.

questions.stem_en: A learner reads a text quickly just to get the general idea. This subskill is ___.
questions.stem_fa: زبان‌آموز متنی را سریع می‌خواند فقط برای گرفتن ایدهٔ کلی. این زیرمهارت ___ است.
questions.explanation_en: Reading quickly for the gist is skimming. Scanning is searching for specific information (a name, a number). Reading for detail is slow, careful reading for full understanding.
questions.explanation_fa: خواندن سریع برای گرفتن کلیت، اسکیمینگ است. اسکنینگ جست‌وجو برای اطلاعات خاص (اسم، عدد) است. خواندن برای جزئیات، خواندنِ آهسته و دقیق برای درک کامل است.

questions.stem_en: Looking through a train timetable to find one departure time is an example of ___.
questions.stem_fa: نگاه‌کردن به جدول قطار برای یافتن یک زمان حرکت، نمونه‌ای از ___ است.
questions.explanation_en: Searching a text for one specific piece of information is scanning. Skimming is for the general idea; inferring is working out meaning that is not directly stated.
questions.explanation_fa: جست‌وجوی متن برای یک تکه اطلاعات خاص، اسکنینگ است. اسکیمینگ برای ایدهٔ کلی است؛ استنتاج یعنی به‌دست‌آوردن معنایی که مستقیم گفته نشده.

questions.stem_en: Linking words such as 'however' and 'because' mainly contribute to a text's ___.
questions.stem_fa: واژه‌های ربط مثل «however» و «because» عمدتاً به ___ متن کمک می‌کنند.
questions.explanation_en: Linking devices grammatically join ideas, creating cohesion. Handwriting and pronunciation are unrelated to how ideas connect in a written text.
questions.explanation_fa: ابزارهای ربط، ایده‌ها را به‌طور دستوری به هم می‌پیوندند و انسجام می‌سازند. دست‌خط و تلفظ ربطی به نحوهٔ اتصال ایده‌ها در متن نوشتاری ندارند.

questions.stem_en: Planning, drafting and editing are stages of the ___ approach to writing.
questions.stem_fa: برنامه‌ریزی، پیش‌نویس و ویرایش، مراحلِ رویکرد ___ به نوشتن‌اند.
questions.explanation_en: Working through stages (plan, draft, revise) is the process approach. The product approach focuses on a final model text. 'Lexical' refers to vocabulary, not a writing approach.
questions.explanation_fa: گذر از مراحل (برنامه، پیش‌نویس، بازنگری) رویکرد فرایندمحور است. رویکرد محصول‌محور بر متن نمونهٔ نهایی تمرکز دارد. «واژگانی» به دایرهٔ واژگان اشاره دارد، نه به رویکرد نوشتن.

questions.stem_en: Listening only to catch the speaker's general idea is listening for ___.
questions.stem_fa: گوش‌دادن فقط برای گرفتن ایدهٔ کلیِ گوینده، گوش‌دادن برای ___ است.
questions.explanation_en: Catching the general idea is listening for gist. Listening for detail means full, careful understanding; listening for specific information means catching one particular item (a time, a price).
questions.explanation_fa: گرفتن ایدهٔ کلی، گوش‌دادن برای کلیت است. گوش‌دادن برای جزئیات یعنی درک کامل و دقیق؛ گوش‌دادن برای اطلاعات خاص یعنی گرفتن یک مورد مشخص (زمان، قیمت).

questions.stem_en: Saying 'What are you doing?' quickly as 'Whatcha doing?' is an example of ___.
questions.stem_fa: گفتنِ سریعِ «What are you doing?» به‌صورت «Whatcha doing?» نمونه‌ای از ___ است.
questions.explanation_en: When words join and weaken in natural speech, that is connected speech — a key reason listening is hard. Intonation is the melody of speech; a minimal pair is two words differing by one sound.
questions.explanation_fa: وقتی واژه‌ها در گفتار طبیعی به هم می‌چسبند و ضعیف می‌شوند، این «گفتار پیوسته» است — یکی از دلایل دشواریِ شنیدن. آهنگ کلام ملودیِ گفتار است؛ جفت کمینه دو واژه است که در یک صدا فرق دارند.

questions.stem_en: Learners chat freely to communicate and the teacher notes errors for later. This activity mainly develops ___.
questions.stem_fa: زبان‌آموزان آزادانه برای ارتباط گپ می‌زنند و معلم خطاها را برای بعد یادداشت می‌کند. این فعالیت عمدتاً ___ را توسعه می‌دهد.
questions.explanation_en: Free communication with delayed correction builds fluency. Accuracy activities focus on producing correct forms during the task; spelling belongs to writing, not a speaking chat.
questions.explanation_fa: ارتباط آزاد با تصحیحِ باتأخیر، روانی می‌سازد. فعالیت‌های دقت روی تولید فرم‌های درست حین تکلیف تمرکز دارند؛ املا به نوشتن مربوط است، نه به گپِ گفتاری.

questions.stem_en: Knowing when to speak and when to let others speak is called ___.
questions.stem_fa: دانستن اینکه کِی صحبت کنیم و کِی به دیگران اجازهٔ صحبت بدهیم، ___ نام دارد.
questions.explanation_en: Managing speaking turns in interaction is turn-taking. Drafting is a writing stage; scanning is a reading subskill.
questions.explanation_fa: مدیریت نوبت‌های گفتار در تعامل، نوبت‌گیری است. پیش‌نویس‌نویسی مرحله‌ای از نوشتن است؛ اسکنینگ زیرمهارتی از خواندن است.

questions.stem_en: A learner studies English because they love films and music in English. This is mainly ___ motivation.
questions.stem_fa: زبان‌آموزی انگلیسی می‌خواند چون عاشق فیلم و موسیقیِ انگلیسی است. این عمدتاً انگیزهٔ ___ است.
questions.explanation_en: Enjoyment and interest from inside is intrinsic motivation. Extrinsic motivation comes from outside rewards (a job, a certificate). The driver here is personal enjoyment, so it is intrinsic.
questions.explanation_fa: لذت و علاقهٔ درونی، انگیزهٔ درونی است. انگیزهٔ بیرونی از پاداش‌های بیرونی (شغل، مدرک) می‌آید. محرکِ اینجا لذتِ شخصی است، پس درونی است.

questions.stem_en: A Persian speaker says 'I have 20 years' instead of 'I am 20'. This error is most likely caused by ___.
questions.stem_fa: فارسی‌زبانی می‌گوید «I have 20 years» به‌جای «I am 20». این خطا به‌احتمال زیاد ناشی از ___ است.
questions.explanation_en: The structure copies the first language's way of stating age, so it is L1 interference. Overgeneralisation would be over-applying an English rule; a slip is a careless one-off the learner can self-correct.
questions.explanation_fa: این ساختار شیوهٔ زبان اول برای گفتنِ سن را کپی می‌کند، پس تداخلِ زبان اول است. تعمیم افراطی یعنی به‌کاربستنِ افراطیِ یک قاعدهٔ انگلیسی؛ لغزش یک اشتباهِ بی‌دقتیِ موردی است که زبان‌آموز خودش اصلاح می‌کند.

questions.stem_en: To check learners understand 'He used to smoke', the best technique is to ___.
questions.stem_fa: برای بررسی اینکه زبان‌آموز «He used to smoke» را فهمیده، بهترین فن ___ است.
questions.explanation_en: CCQs (e.g. 'Does he smoke now? No') check meaning reliably. 'Do you understand?' often gets 'yes' even when learners do not. Translation is not always available or precise.
questions.explanation_fa: CCQها (مثل «الان سیگار می‌کشد؟ نه») معنا را مطمئن می‌سنجند. «فهمیدید؟» اغلب «بله» می‌گیرد حتی وقتی نفهمیده‌اند. ترجمه همیشه در دسترس یا دقیق نیست.

questions.stem_en: A test given on the first day to place students in the right class is a ___.
questions.stem_fa: آزمونی که روز اول برای قراردادنِ دانش‌آموز در کلاسِ درست گرفته می‌شود، ___ است.
questions.explanation_en: A placement test sorts learners into the right level. An achievement test checks what was taught on a course; a proficiency test measures overall ability regardless of any course.
questions.explanation_fa: آزمون تعیین‌سطح زبان‌آموز را در سطحِ درست قرار می‌دهد. آزمون دستاورد آنچه در دوره آموزش داده شد را می‌سنجد؛ آزمون بسندگی توانایی کلی را فارغ از هر دوره اندازه می‌گیرد.

questions.stem_en: Which is the best-written lesson aim?
questions.stem_fa: کدام هدفِ درس بهتر نوشته شده است؟

options.text_en: Learners will be able to talk about life experiences using the present perfect.
options.text_fa: زبان‌آموز خواهد توانست با حال کامل دربارهٔ تجربه‌های زندگی صحبت کند.

questions.explanation_en: A good aim is learner-focused and states what learners will be able to DO. A describes the teacher's action; C describes an activity, not a learning outcome.
questions.explanation_fa: هدفِ خوب زبان‌آموزمحور است و می‌گوید زبان‌آموز چه خواهد توانست انجام دهد. گزینهٔ A کنشِ معلم را می‌گوید؛ گزینهٔ C یک فعالیت را می‌گوید، نه یک نتیجهٔ یادگیری.

questions.stem_en: In a lesson plan, predicting that a sound will be hard and preparing a solution is called ___.
questions.stem_fa: در طرح درس، پیش‌بینی اینکه یک صدا سخت خواهد بود و آماده‌کردنِ راه‌حل، ___ نام دارد.
questions.explanation_en: Foreseeing difficulties and planning solutions is 'anticipated problems'. Timetable fit is how the lesson connects to the course; interaction pattern is who works with whom.
questions.explanation_fa: پیش‌بینیِ دشواری‌ها و برنامه‌ریزیِ راه‌حل، «مشکلاتِ پیش‌بینی‌شده» است. تناسب با برنامه یعنی اتصالِ درس به دوره؛ الگوی تعامل یعنی چه‌کسی با چه‌کسی کار می‌کند.

questions.stem_en: Bringing back language from earlier lessons so it is not forgotten is called ___.
questions.stem_fa: بازگرداندنِ زبان از درس‌های قبلی تا فراموش نشود، ___ نام دارد.
questions.explanation_en: Deliberately re-using earlier language across a sequence is recycling. Eliciting draws language from learners; drilling is repetition practice of a form.
questions.explanation_fa: استفادهٔ عمدیِ دوبارهٔ زبانِ پیشین در طولِ یک توالی، بازآموزی است. استخراج زبان را از زبان‌آموز بیرون می‌کشد؛ drilling تمرینِ تکراریِ یک فرم است.

questions.stem_en: A reading text in the coursebook is too hard, so the teacher rewrites it more simply. This is ___.
questions.stem_fa: یک متنِ خواندنِ کتاب درسی خیلی سخت است، پس معلم آن را ساده‌تر بازنویسی می‌کند. این ___ است.
questions.explanation_en: Changing existing material to fit the class is adapting. Omitting means leaving it out; supplementing means adding extra material on top.
questions.explanation_fa: تغییرِ مطلبِ موجود برای تناسب با کلاس، تطبیق است. حذف یعنی کنارگذاشتنِ آن؛ تکمیل یعنی افزودنِ مطلبِ اضافه.

questions.stem_en: A real restaurant menu used in a lesson is an example of ___.
questions.stem_fa: منوی واقعیِ یک رستوران که در درس استفاده می‌شود، نمونه‌ای از ___ است.
questions.explanation_en: A real text not made for teaching (a menu) is authentic material / realia. A coursebook is made for teaching; a placement test is an assessment, not material like this.
questions.explanation_fa: متنِ واقعی که برای آموزش ساخته نشده (منو) مطلبِ اصیل / realia است. کتاب درسی برای آموزش ساخته شده؛ آزمون تعیین‌سطح یک ارزیابی است، نه چنین مطلبی.

questions.stem_fa: کدام یک وسیلهٔ کمک‌آموزشی است؟
questions.explanation_en: An interactive whiteboard is a tool that supports teaching — a teaching aid. A lesson aim is a goal; a learning style is a learner characteristic.
questions.explanation_fa: تختهٔ هوشمند ابزاری است که از تدریس پشتیبانی می‌کند — یک وسیلهٔ کمک‌آموزشی. هدفِ درس یک مقصود است؛ سبکِ یادگیری یک ویژگیِ زبان‌آموز است.

questions.stem_en: Simplifying your own language so learners at a lower level can understand you is called ___.
questions.stem_fa: ساده‌کردنِ زبانِ خودت تا زبان‌آموزِ سطحِ پایین‌تر بفهمد، ___ نام دارد.
questions.explanation_en: Adjusting your language to the learners' level is grading language. Eliciting draws answers from learners; drilling is repetition of a form.
questions.explanation_fa: تنظیمِ زبانت متناسب با سطحِ زبان‌آموز، grading language است. استخراج پاسخ را از زبان‌آموز می‌گیرد؛ drilling تکرارِ یک فرم است.

questions.stem_en: A learner writes 'libary' for 'library'. By type, this mistake is best described as ___.
questions.stem_fa: زبان‌آموزی «libary» را به‌جای «library» می‌نویسد. از نظرِ نوع، این اشتباه بهتر است ___ توصیف شود.
questions.explanation_en: The word and grammar are correct; only the written form is wrong, so it is a spelling mistake. A grammar mistake breaks a rule of form; a lexical mistake uses the wrong word.
questions.explanation_fa: واژه و دستور درست‌اند؛ فقط شکلِ نوشتاری غلط است، پس اشتباهِ املایی است. اشتباهِ دستوری قاعدهٔ فرم را می‌شکند؛ اشتباهِ واژگانی واژهٔ غلط به‌کار می‌برد.

questions.stem_en: The teacher wants every learner to speak as much as possible in a short time. The best grouping is usually ___.
questions.stem_fa: معلم می‌خواهد هر زبان‌آموز در زمانِ کوتاه تا حدِ ممکن صحبت کند. بهترین گروه‌بندی معمولاً ___ است.

options.text_en: the teacher asking one student at a time
options.text_fa: معلم هر بار از یک نفر می‌پرسد

questions.explanation_en: Pairwork lets many learners speak at once, maximising speaking time. Asking one student at a time means only one speaks; silent individual work has no speaking at all.
questions.explanation_fa: کارِ جفتی به زبان‌آموزانِ زیادی اجازه می‌دهد هم‌زمان صحبت کنند و زمانِ صحبت را بیشینه می‌کند. پرسیدن از یک نفر در هر بار یعنی فقط یک نفر صحبت می‌کند؛ کارِ فردیِ ساکت اصلاً صحبت ندارد.

questions.stem_en: Which is the most effective piece of feedback?
questions.stem_fa: کدام بازخورد مؤثرتر است؟

options.text_en: Great use of the past tense — just say 'went', not 'goed'.
options.text_fa: کاربردِ خوبِ گذشته — فقط بگو 'went' نه 'goed'.

questions.explanation_en: Effective feedback is specific and balanced: it praises something real and gives one clear correction. 'Good job!' is vague; 'That was wrong' is demotivating and unhelpful.
questions.explanation_fa: بازخوردِ مؤثر مشخص و متوازن است: چیزِ واقعی‌ای را تحسین می‌کند و یک تصحیحِ روشن می‌دهد. «آفرین!» مبهم است؛ «غلط بود» انگیزه‌زدا و بی‌فایده است.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: The likely time different activities or stages in a lesson plan should take. When teachers plan lessons, they think about how long each activity will take and they usually write this on their plan.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Timing”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Timing» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: Using your experience or knowledge to say what you think will happen in the future. Prediction is a technique or learning strategy learners can use to help with listening or reading. Learners think about the topic before they read or listen. They try to imagine what the topic will be or what they are going to read about or listen to, using clues like headlines or pictures accompanying the text or their general knowledge about the text type or topic. This makes it easier for learners to understand what they read or hear.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Prediction”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Prediction» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: A phrasal verb in which the particle can be separated from the verb, e.g. He looked up a word. / He looked a word up. See phrasal verb.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Separable”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Separable» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: A set of actions that describes the way to do something. Teachers write lesson plans and provide details of exactly what is going to happen in each stage of a lesson; e.g. learners practise the language of complaints in a role-play in pairs. The details of the different actions are the procedures of the lesson.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Procedure”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Procedure» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: A learner who learns more easily by doing things physically. This type of learner may like to move around or move objects while learning.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Kinaesthetic learner”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Kinaesthetic learner» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: A model of a person or animal often made of paper or cloth, which a teacher or learners can move by putting their hand inside. Puppets are often used when teaching young learners.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Puppet”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Puppet» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: An approach to teaching in which a teacher provides examples of the target language and then guides the learners to work out the language rules for themselves. For example, learners read an article which has examples of reported speech. Learners find the examples and answer questions about the grammar rules and the meaning of the examples.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Guided discovery”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Guided discovery» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: To help learners to improve their listening, reading, writing and speaking ability. Teachers do this in class by providing activities which focus on skills development; e.g. learners read a text and answer comprehension questions. See skills.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Develop skills”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Develop skills» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: An utterance which corrects or modifies what has just been said e.g. Can you tell me where I can, um, send, um, I mean, post this letter? Using Um and I mean are this learner’s repair strategy.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Repair strategy”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Repair strategy» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: To reword a sentence or phrase to improve it, e.g. when a teacher corrects what a learner has said by repeating the sentence correctly, but without drawing the learner’s attention to the mistake. This is usually the way parents ‘correct’ their young children’s language mistakes. Learner: I am not agree. Teacher:  Oh, you don’t agree. Why not?
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Recast”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Recast» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: When learners try to understand how and why a particular piece of language is used or how it is formed. For example, learners read a text with different past tenses, then look at the example sentences in the text and work out how the different tenses are used and how they are formed. See deduce meaning from context.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Work out”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Work out» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: A task-type in which learners read a text with missing words and try to work out what the missing words are. The missing words are removed regularly from the text, e.g. every seventh word. A cloze test is used for testing reading ability or general language use. It is different from a gap-fill activity, which can focus on practising or testing a specific language point and particular words connected to the language point are removed from the text. See gap-fill.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Cloze test”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Cloze test» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: A method or approach to teaching in which the teacher presents language items in instructions and the learners have to do exactly what the teacher tells them; e.g. the teacher says: Stand up and the learners stand up; the teacher says: Walk to the window and the learners walk to a window in the classroom.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Total Physical Response (TPR)”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Total Physical Response (TPR)» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: Understanding something which is spoken or written. Teachers give learners comprehension tasks to help them understand listening and reading texts or to assess understanding.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Comprehension”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Comprehension» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: 1. To watch and listen to learners when they are working on their own or in pairs or groups in order to make sure that they are doing what they have been asked to do, and to help them if they are having problems. For example, while learners are doing a role-play in pairs, the teacher walks around the room listening to them, perhaps noting down errors, and helping when needed. 2. To listen to or read the language you use to check if it is accurate and effective. Teachers do this to make sure that learners can understand them. See teacher role.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Monitor”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Monitor» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: To get advice or information from someone or something; e.g. teachers and learners might consult a dictionary or grammar book.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Consult”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Consult» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: A particular form of something in which some details are different from an earlier or later form of it; e.g. a written text may have different versions; two people might tell a different version of the same story.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Version”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Version» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: Clarifying language. Correcting learner language. Consolidating learner language. Contextualising language. Eliciting language. Explaining language. Modelling language. Checking understanding of language. Personalising language. Providing language input.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Language resource”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Language resource» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: To teach someone to do a particular job or activity. For example, John trains people to sing. It also means to study or learn to do something. For example, I trained to be a teacher.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Train”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Train» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: Scaffolding is the temporary support that teachers (and parents) give to learners to help them to do a task, solve a problem, communicate or understand. Scaffolding can be through the use of teacher language to help learners understand language and use of language, e.g. using language at the learners’ level; asking questions; using gestures and actions when speaking; using L1 when necessary. Scaffolding can also be through the use of teaching strategies, e.g. providing language models or prompts; using substitution tables and language frames. Scaffolding is temporary support which is gradually taken away so that learners can eventually work without it.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Scaffolding”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Scaffolding» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: A way of categorising words according to their grammatical function and meaning, e.g. noun, verb, adjective, pronoun, adverb, preposition, conjunction. Similar to word class. See word class.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Part of speech”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Part of speech» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: To listen to or read a text in order to understand most of what it says; e.g. learners listening for detail to someone talking about a their last holiday would have a task to listen for where the holiday was, when it was, how long it was, what things the person did etc. See gist, global understanding.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Detail”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Detail» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: In connected speech many words are not pronounced fully. For example, prepositions, auxiliary verbs, pronouns and conjunctions are usually not pronounced fully and are not stressed. This way of teaching target language can be helpful if the teacher thinks the learners may already know some of the target language. questions.passage:
A mistake that a learner makes when trying to say or write something above their level of language or language processing. A developmental error is an error made by a second language learner which could also be made by a child learning their mother tongue as part of their normal development. A second language learner might make the error because they are applying a rule they have learned that doesn’t work for this particular case e.g. I goed there last week (I went there last week). A fossilised error is an error that has become (almost) permanent in a learner’s language and has become a 
habit. Fossilised errors cannot easily be corrected. For example, a B2 learner might habitually not add an ‘s’ 
when saying third person singular present simple verbs. Learners at this level do not usually make this 
mistake, but, for this learner, the error was not corrected early and it has become habitual. See fossilisation. A slip. When a learner makes a slip they make a language mistake but they are able to correct themselves, 
e.g. Learner: He like ice-cream, I mean, he likes ice-cream.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Error”. The other options are different ELT terms.

questions.passage:
What the teacher wants to achieve in the lesson or in the course. The main aim is the most important aim; e.g. the teacher’s main aim in a lesson could be to teach the present perfect simple or develop listening skills. A stage aim is the aim or purpose of a stage, step or short section of a lesson, e.g. to provide controlled 
practice of the present perfect simple or to develop listening for gist. A subsidiary aim is the secondary focus of the lesson, less important than the main aim. It could be the 
language or skills learners use in order to achieve the main aim of the lesson, or a skill or language area 
which is practised while the teacher is working on achieving the main lesson aim. A personal aim is what the teacher would like to improve in his/her teaching, e.g. to reduce the time I spend 
writing on the whiteboard.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: Language Across the Curriculum.  In the UK this refers to the use of language in the L1 medium curriculum.  Elsewhere it refers to subject teaching in a target language.  (e.g. US, Jamaica) language demands:  the language abilities which a learner needs in order to be able to use a language for learning in a given subject, during a subject lesson or using a given subject textbook. Lessons, subjects, textbooks, information technology therefore make language demands on learners.  Teachers need knowledge of the language demands in order to support the learning of subject concepts. language demands analysis:  the analysis which a subject (or language) teacher makes of the language demands which a subject lesson or textbook etc will make on a class.  It is part of lesson planning in CLIL.  On the basis of this analysis, a teacher can decide where in a given lesson a learner will need language support. language needs:  the language needs which specific learners in any group have with respect to a given subject, lesson, textbook or website.  A subject lesson therefore makes language demands on a whole class; whereas individuals in the class have individual language needs with respect to those demands. language showers:  regular, short, continuous exposure to CLIL delivered in the target language for 15 or 30 minutes several times a week.  They are often associated with Primary schools and are usually taught in one subject area. learners:  CLIL covers primary, secondary and tertiary contexts.  Learners, rather than students or pupils, describes this age range. medium of instruction:  language used as medium for school learning. partial immersion:  usually 50-60% of curriculum subjects taught in target language (regions of Spain, Egypt) target language:  the CLIL language. Part 1 and Part 2 of TKT: CLIL Module. accommodation:  (assessment) adapting tasks,  materials, timing or test procedure so that learners can understand what they have to do and can demonstrate what they know. activating prior / previous knowledge:  encouraging learners to produce language or ideas about what they already know about a subject before it is taught. (e.g.  Tell me six words connected with ‘electricity’. Think of three sources of electricity.) analysis:  examining something in detail so that it can be understood. animation:  making many images so that they appear to move on a screen e.g. computer animation. anticipated problems:  problems which teachers think learners could face during a lesson. These could be problems with understanding content, language or new concepts approach:  the different theories about learning and how languages are learned e.g. the communicative approach. assessment criteria:  assessment criteria are the statements written in order to judge how well a learner has achieved the learning outcomes. For example, • learning outcome: to describe the life-cycle of a butterfly. • criteria: the number of stages in the life cycle the learner correctly describes and how well the learner links them.

questions.passage: Teachers helping learners to make what they write or say better or right. Echo correction – When learners make a mistake, the teacher repeats the mistake with rising intonation encouraging learners to correct themselves, e.g. Learner: He don’t like it. Teacher: Don’t? Learner: He doesn’t like it. Finger correction – A way of drawing attention to where a learner has made a mistake. The teacher counts out the words a learner has said on her fingers. The fingers represent words and the teacher can show clearly in which word (finger) the mistake was made. A teacher may use  finger correction to show that a mistake has been made with word or sentence stress, word order, grammar, pronunciation of sounds etc. Peer correction – When learners correct each other’s mistakes, perhaps with some help from the teacher. Self-correction – When learners correct language mistakes they have made, perhaps with some help from the teacher. See ignore (errors).

questions.passage: A technique teachers use to provide learners with practice of language. It involves guided repetition of words or sentences. In a choral drill the teacher says a word or sentence and the learners repeat it together as a class. In an individual drill the teacher says a word or sentence and one learner repeats it. In a substitution drill the teacher provides a sentence and a different word or phrase which the learner(s) must use (or substitute) in exactly the same structure, e.g. Teacher: I bought a book. Pen Learner(s): I bought a pen. In a transformation drill the teacher says a word or a sentence and the learner answers by changing the sentence into a new grammatical structure, e.g. Teacher: I bought a pen. Didn’t Learner: I didn’t buy a pen. Teacher: I went to the cinema. Didn’t Learner: I didn’t go to the cinema.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: A verb form that is used for a possible or imagined situation. Grammar books often mention five kinds of conditionals: Zero conditional – is used when we talk about something that is always true if another action takes place, e.g. If it rains, the ground gets wet. First (Type 1) conditional – is used for present or future possible or likely situations, e.g. I will come if I can. Second (Type 2) conditional – is used for present or future situations which the speaker thinks are impossible or unlikely, e.g. I would play for West Ham United if they asked me. Third (Type 3) conditional – is used for past situations that cannot be changed, e.g. I would have seen her if I had arrived earlier (but I didn’t so I couldn’t). Mixed conditional – is used when the speaker wants to talk about different time frames in one sentence, e.g. If I’d arrived on time, I wouldn’t have to wait now. ‘If I’d arrived’ is about the past and ‘I wouldn’t have to wait’ is about the present.

questions.passage: Part of a lesson when the teacher discusses ideas with the whole class; for example a plenary could be held at the end of a lesson when the teacher might assess learning by asking learners to review what has been learned. See open class.

questions.passage: To choose and name one learner to speak or do a particular task. Teachers nominate different learners to give the answers to a task in order to ensure that more learners get a chance to speak.

questions.passage: When a teacher decides whether a learner is doing well or not, or whether a course is successful or not, by evaluating learners by thinking about their strengths and weaknesses and thinking about their progress rather than setting a test or writing an official report. See formal assessment.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Informal assessment”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Informal assessment» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: A phrase that is added to the end of a sentence to make it a question, or to check that someone agrees with the statement just made, e.g. It’s very cold, isn’t it? It isn’t very far, is it?
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Question tag, tag question”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Question tag, tag question» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: When learners assess themselves, they decide how good they think their progress, learning or language use is. See peer assessment.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Self-assessment”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Self-assessment» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: To ask someone (politely) to do something, e.g. Please could you open the window?
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Request”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Request» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: A list of key words learners need to know in order to understand a text or to talk about a topic. Teachers might use a word bank to pre-teach vocabulary for a text, or they might provide learners with a word bank to use while they are reading a text as a way of supporting their reading.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Word bank”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Word bank» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage:
Closed pairs – When learners in the class work with the person sitting next to them but not in front of the class. 
For example, learners discuss the answers to a task with the person sitting next to them. Open pairs – In open pairs, one pair does an activity in front of the class. This technique is useful for showing 
how to do an activity and/or for focusing on accuracy.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Pairs”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Pairs» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: An indirect question is used when someone wants to ask something in a more polite way, e.g. I was wondering if you could help me. (indirect question) instead of Could you help me? (direct question). See direct speech and reported speech.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Indirect question”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Indirect question» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: To explain or describe something in a clear and exact way. Teachers specify lesson aims in their lesson plans. Aims are specified at the beginning of a lesson plan for the lesson as a whole and for individual stages in the lesson.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Specify”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Specify» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: A word or phrase that indicates time, such as after, last weekend, e.g. I will meet you after the lesson.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Time expression”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Time expression» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: To make something possible. Teachers facilitate learning by planning and delivering lessons, maintaining discipline in the classroom and making it easier for learners to learn. See teacher role.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Facilitate”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Facilitate» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: When a learner can set his/her own aims and organise his/her own study, they are autonomous and independent. Many activities in coursebooks help learners to be more independent by encouraging them to find out more about things in the book and helping them to organise their learning, such as by suggesting they keep vocabulary lists. See learning strategies, learner training.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Learner autonomy”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Learner autonomy» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: When we study language, we study words, sentences or whole texts. When we study words in a text we are studying language at word level, studying sentences in a text is studying language at sentence level, and when we study whole texts we are studying language at text level. Studying a text at word level means looking at the language features of words in the text. For example, looking at the part of speech, the spelling, the register etc. of words in a text. See sentence level, text level.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Word level”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Word level» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: A number of different things which are of the same type.  For example, teachers can use a range of activities in their lessons such as a discussion activity, a role-play activity, multiple choice questions, or a gap fill activity.  We also use range when we talk about learners’ language. For example, when a learner uses many different adjectives in a description of her town we say she has ‘a good range of adjectives’ whereas when a learner uses just a few adjectives again and again when describing her town we say that she has ‘a limited range of adjectives’.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Range”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Range» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: 1. To make clear what you mean, e.g. to repeat something using clearer words or say something again in a clearer way. See ask for clarification. 2. Clarify language. When teachers focus on form, meaning and pronunciation in a lesson to help learners understand the use and rules of target language. For example, showing learners that the past perfect is made of had + the past participle, that it’s used for an earlier past action and telling them that had can be written ’d is clarifying language.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Clarify”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Clarify» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: To learn a language without studying it, just by hearing and/or reading and then using it. This is the way we learn our first language. See acquisition, exposure.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Pick up”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Pick up» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: When the teacher leads activities in the classroom, usually from the front of the class with the learners’ attention on him/her. In teacher-centred parts of the lesson the teacher might be explaining something to the learners or asking individual learners questions or asking learners to repeat models or giving instructions. If a lesson is thought to be too teacher-centred it means that learners are not being given opportunities to work together, to practise, share experiences or think for themselves. See learner-centred.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Teacher-centred”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Teacher-centred» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: A classroom activity in which the teacher describes a scene or an object and learners draw what they hear. The activity can also be for learners to describe a scene or an object and other learners draw what they hear, perhaps in pairs; e.g. learner A describes and learner B draws. See listen and do/make/draw.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Picture dictation”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Picture dictation» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: To pay attention to something, to notice something, to highlight something; e.g. teachers might focus on words in a text the learners are reading by giving learners a task which helps them to understand the meanings of the words.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Focus on”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Focus on» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: To introduce different things such as different types of activities or tasks, language skills, interaction patterns, pacing or timing into a lesson. Teachers try to include variety in their lessons, so that learners stay interested.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Variety”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Variety» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: 1. A sound in which the air is partly blocked by the lips, tongue, teeth etc., e.g. /θ/ in ‘thing’, /b/ in ‘boy’. See diphthong and vowel. 2. Any letter of the English alphabet which represents consonant sounds, e.g. d  /d/, c  /k/.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Consonant”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Consonant» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: Written or spoken texts which a first language speaker might read or listen to. They may be taken from newspapers, radio, the internet etc. The language in the texts is not adapted or made easier for learners or the language learning process.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Authentic material”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Authentic material» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: The materials which teachers and learners can use to find or check information, e.g. grammar books, dictionaries and online teaching resources.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Reference materials”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Reference materials» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: To put things into the group to which they belong. For example, learners might categorise a list of different foods into groups such as fruit and vegetables.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Categorise”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Categorise» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: To show what something means by giving examples or using visual aids. Teachers sometimes use pictures to show learners what new words mean.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Illustrate meaning”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Illustrate meaning» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: To shorten or change or correct the words or content of some parts of a written text to make it clearer or easier to understand; e.g. learners might edit the first draft of a text they have written to correct the mistakes. See process writing.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Edit”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Edit» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: The written version of the words learners hear when doing a listening activity. These can often be found in a teacher’s book or at the back of the learner’s book.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Tapescript, audio script, transcript”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Tapescript, audio script, transcript» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: 1. A short activity between the main stages of a lesson used for reasons such as time management or to provide a change of pace etc. For example, learners do a word game after a difficult piece of reading before moving on to some grammar work. 2. A word or sound used between words or sentences in spoken English when someone is thinking of what to say; e.g. When I went to London … um … I think it was about … er … 4 years ago. Er and um are fillers.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Filler”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Filler» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: Words or phrases that complete the meaning of another word or a sentence; e.g. in the sentence He gave the man a ticket, ‘the man a ticket’ is the complement. In Jane was unavailable, ‘unavailable’ is the complement.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Complement”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Complement» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: To decide how a writer or speaker feels about something from the way that they speak or write, rather than from what they openly say. Teachers might help learners to infer attitude by helping them to understand intonation. For example, learners could listen to a recording and say if they think the person is happy or sad.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Infer attitude, feeling, mood”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Infer attitude, feeling, mood» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: A sound produced by air passing through the nose, e.g. /ŋ/, /m/.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Nasal”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Nasal» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: To repeat a phrase, sentence, rhyme, verse, poem or song, usually with others, in a regular rhythm. Teachers use chants to practise pronunciation and to help learners remember vocabulary.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Chant”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Chant» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: To read or listen to a text and understand the general meaning of it, without paying attention to specific details – for example, reading a restaurant review quickly to find out if the writer liked the restaurant or not. See detail, read for detail, listen for detail, intensive listening/reading, scan, skim.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Gist”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Gist» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: When a learner tells the whole class what was discussed in groupwork or pairwork. For example, after a group discussion on using the internet, one of the learners in each group tells the whole class the main points mentioned in their group.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Report back”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Report back» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: An example of a grammar point, function or lexical set; e.g. Can you open the window, please? is an exponent for making requests.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Exponent”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Exponent» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: A task-type in which learners are given parts of a sentence, e.g. the beginning or the end, and are asked to complete the sentence, using specific target language, e.g. to practise using –ing forms: At the weekend, I love … ; In the evenings, I enjoy … .
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Sentence completion activity”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Sentence completion activity» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: A relative clause in which the relative pronoun and possibly the auxiliary verb have been left out; e.g. in the person I saw on the bus, ‘who’ is left out (the person who I saw on the bus); in the house hit by the storm, ‘which was’ is left out (the house which was hit by the storm).
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Reduced relative clause”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Reduced relative clause» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: An approach to writing, which thinks of writing as a process which includes different stages of writing such as planning, drafting, re-drafting, editing, proofreading. See guided writing and product writing.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Process writing”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Process writing» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: This involves teachers thinking about learners’ classroom performance to assess how well learners communicate during specific tasks by checking learners’ performance against criteria. Teachers can see if learners have achieved the purpose of the task by using the criteria.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Performance assessment”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Performance assessment» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: 1. The situation in which language is used or presented; e.g. a story about a holiday experience could be used as the context to present and practise past tenses. Photographs can help to provide a context for a magazine article. 2. The words or phrases before or after a word in discourse which help someone to understand that word, e.g. I drove my van to the town centre and parked it in the car park. questions.passage: Pronouncing part of a word (syllable) or part of a sentence louder and longer than other parts, e.g. VEGetable, I LOVE baNAnas. Some parts of words and sentences are stressed and some are unstressed. Contrastive stress is used when we compare or contrast and involves stressing the word we are comparing or contrasting, e.g. It was my AUNT who bought the car (not my uncle) or My aunt bought the CAKE (not the biscuits)! Primary, main stress Some sentences and longer words may have more than one part stressed, These words and sentences have a primary or main stress which is the part that is loudest and longest. For example, pronunciation – prəˌnʌn.siˈeɪ.ʃən. The main stress is on the /eɪ/ part of the word. The primary or main stress on a word is marked in the dictionary with e.g. prəˌnʌn.siˈeɪ.ʃən. Secondary stress is stress on a syllable or word in a sentence which is less strong than the primary (main) stress, e.g. /prəˌnʌn.siˈeɪ.ʃən/ has the primary or main stress on /eɪ/ and the secondary stress on /ˌnʌn/. Secondary stress is marked in the dictionary with e.g. prəˌnʌn.siˈeɪ.ʃən. Sentence stress is about the way some words in a sentence are stressed and some are unstressed. The stressed words are usually the information-carrying words or content words such as nouns, verbs, adjectives and adverbs. For example, It was a lovely evening, and the temperature was perfect. Word stress is about which syllable of a word is pronounced louder and longer – e.g. umBRELLa /ʌmˈbrelə/.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage:
A formal assessment of a learner’s language. An achievement test is used to see how well learners have learned the language and skills taught in class. Achievement tests are often at the end of term or end of the year and test the main points of what has been taught in that time. A diagnostic test is used to identify problems that learners have with language or skills. The teacher 
diagnoses or finds out the language problems learners have. It can also be used to diagnose or find out about 
learner strengths. Diagnostic tests help the teacher to plan what to teach, or what not to teach, in future. An objective test is a test which has only one correct answer (for each question) and is marked without using 
the examiner’s opinion, e.g. true/false questions, multiple-choice questions. There is a clear right or wrong 
answer. A placement test is used at the beginning of a course in a language school in order to identify a learner’s 
level of language and find the best class for them. A proficiency test is used to see how good learners are at using the target language. The contents of a 
proficiency test are not chosen according to what has been taught, but according to what is needed for a 
particular purpose, e.g. English for hotel receptionists, English for studying at university, English for general 
communication. Cambridge English: First and IELTS are examples of proficiency tests. A progress test is used during a course in order to assess the learning up to a particular point in the course. 
Teachers might set progress tests weekly or in the middle of a course. A subjective test is a test in which the examiner’s opinion and judgement is used to decide on the quality of 
the answer. There isn’t just one correct answer.  There may be many different answers which are correct. 
Types of subjective test are, for example; written stories, compositions, interviews, conversations. Subjective 
tests can be made more reliable by using assessment criteria. See assessment criteria. A summative test is used at the end of a course. The focus is on the mark or grade given and feedback is 
not usually provided. See formative assessment.
questions.explanation_en: The definition describes “Test”. The other options are different ELT terms.
questions.explanation_fa: این تعریف، «Test» را توصیف می‌کند؛ گزینه‌های دیگر اصطلاحاتِ متفاوتی هستند.

questions.passage: What a word expresses. There are several kinds of meaning. Denotation noun The dictionary definition of a word, e.g. a chair is a piece of furniture with legs and we use it to sit on. See Literal. Figurative adjective An imaginative meaning of a word, e.g. he put all his heart into his new job – ‘heart’ has the figurative meaning of making a lot of effort. Literal adjective The original or basic meaning of a word rather than an imaginative or poetic meaning, e.g. the literal meaning of heart – the organ in your chest that sends blood around your body. See denotation.

questions.stem_fa: کدام اصطلاح با این تعریف مطابقت دارد؟
questions.passage: student talking time. subject-led CLIL:  when curricular subjects are learned through a non-native language The subject content is the focus of the language learned. subject-specific language:  the language needed for particular curriculum subjects e.g. PE:  match, ball, coordination, footwork. support:  ways to help and guide learners as they receive and produce new content and language e.g. teachers grading language; simplifying texts; using visuals, word banks, sentence starters, writing frames and visual organisers. syllabus:  this describes the curricular subjects to be covered during a course and the order in which they will be taught. synthesis:  (see creative thinking) task differentiation:  changing tasks so that some learners have easier materials or questions while others have more challenging materials or questions. technical terms: vocabulary specific to that subject area and unlikely to appear anywhere else e.g. ‘Impressionism ’ in Art (see specialist vocabulary) technique:  how teachers use different classroom activities e.g. using a brainstorming activity to activate prior knowledge. text level:  the discourse features of a text e.g. an instruction text: statement about what is to be made, a list of items needed, steps written in chronological order, use of visuals or diagrams.  (see also word level and sentence level) the 4Cs:  content, communication, cognition and culture (Coyle, 1999).  These are interrelated components of CLIL.  Culture is also linked to citizenship and community. theory:  a set of ideas which explains something, for example mathematical, musical or scientific theory. thought bubbles:  used to show what someone is thinking. timeline:  see visual organisers. transfer:  the ability to apply an idea or a skill that has been learnt in one context and to use it in a different context. variable:  (science experiments) an amount or a condition which can change while other amounts or conditions stay the same.  For example, testing which plants grow well: same soil, same type of seed, same amount of water but different amount of light. visual organisers / graphic organisers:  aids such as diagrams and charts which are used to help learners remember new information by making thinking visual.  They involve writing down or drawing ideas and making connections.  Organisers can be simple or complex but all of them have connecting parts.  There are several common patterns: 1.  bar chart 10.  process or cause-effect diagram 2.  Carroll diagram 11.  quadrants (sounds) Y not Y X not X high low loud quiet 3.  cycle 12.  storyboard 4.  flow diagram 13.  T-chart 5.  grid 14.  table 6.  key (binary) 15.  timeline 7.  line graph 16.  tree diagram . . . . . . . Yes No Are the cones round? Does it have pointed leaves? Yes No Yes No e.g. Trees: Does it have cones? 0.00 0.20 0.40 0.60 0.80 1.00 Line 1 Line 2 Line 3 8.  mind map  (descriptive) 17.  Venn diagrams 9.  pie chart 18  Venn diagram . . . . . Topic Visual organisers Purpose of organiser 1.  bar chart To show frequency using rectangles which are the same width 2.  Carroll diagram To classify information according two sets of different criteria 3.  cycle To show a series of events which happen again and again in the same order 4.  flow diagram (or chart) To represent information to show the steps in a process or in making a decision 5.  grid: squares in rows and columns To show locations of places e.g. on maps 6.  key: binary To divide information into two parts using a series of questions, each of which has only two possible answers. 7.  line graph To show a trend or data using  X and Y axes 8.  mind map (descriptive) To show facts about specific people, places, objects or events. 9.  pie chart To show different amounts of frequencies as parts of a circle 10.  process diagram or cause/ effect diagram To show the cause effect factors which lead to an outcome or a sequence of steps which leads to the manufacture of a product 11.  quadrants To show connections between two sets of concepts 12.  storyboard To show the events in a story sometimes with speech and thought bubbles or short text. 13.  T-chart To show two sides of a topic such as for and against an argument; advantages and disadvantages; facts and opinions 14.  table To categorise information usually with columns and headings 15.  time line To show events in chronological order. 16.  tree diagram To classify words and show their relationships often with examples 17.  Venn diagrams To show similarities and differences between two or among three objects, people, concepts, places etc. 18.  Venn diagram To show a whole set of items and a sub-set of those items volunteer: 1.  (noun) a person who offers to help. 2.  (verb) to offer to help someone.  For example, a learner could volunteer to show another learner how to create a PowerPoint presentation. wait time:  teachers wait for several seconds before learners respond to questions rather than expecting an immediate response.  In CLIL, some people say teachers should wait about 7 seconds in order to allow learners to process their thoughts in a non-native language. word bank:  a list of key words required for learning subject concepts.  It can be used to pre- teach, to support input and to help learners remember key subject vocabulary. word level:  language features of a text such as the use of technical vocabulary, adjectives, quantities. (see also sentence level and text level)

questions.explanation_en: “simplifying a hard reading text” is an example of Using coursebook materials.
